task_completion.description: The agent modified parser.py to correctly handle operator precedence.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In PyCharm or terminal, parser.py is seen with new logic (like a parse_term method) that evaluates '*' and '/' before '+' and '-'.

task_completion.description: The agent fixed the CSV type inference bug in csv_io.py or the empty cell bug in cell.py.
task_completion.visual_evidence: csv_io.py shows .strip() being used before checking isdigit() or float(), OR cell.py shows get_numeric_value() returning None instead of 0 for empty cells.

task_completion.description: The agent fixed the false circular dependency detection in dependency.py.
task_completion.visual_evidence: dependency.py shows the use of two separate sets (e.g., 'visited' and 'in_progress', or 'permanent' and 'temporary') in get_evaluation_order() to correctly detect cycles.

task_completion.description: The agent implemented 2D range evaluation in evaluator.py.
task_completion.visual_evidence: evaluator.py shows eval_range() with logic to handle 2D ranges, such as nested loops for rows and columns, or parsing ranges like A1:C3.

task_completion.description: The agent implemented the missing functions (VLOOKUP, COUNTIF, AVERAGE) in functions.py.
task_completion.visual_evidence: functions.py shows code replacing 'raise NotImplementedError' in func_vlookup, func_countif, or func_average with actual implementation logic.

task_completion.description: The agent ran the test suite and achieved at least 30 passing tests.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing a pytest run with a result indicating >= 30 tests passed out of 35 (e.g., '30 passed, 5 failed', '35 passed', etc.).

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not modify files in tests/ or data/.
integrity.visual_evidence: No open tabs or terminal commands showing edits to files in the tests/ or data/ directories.

integrity.description: Ensure the test results are from an actual pytest execution.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal shows the standard pytest output format (e.g., 'collected 35 items', '======= 35 passed in ... =======') and is not just a printed string.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent didn't hardcode specific test answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code in the engine files contains general logic, not hardcoded values like 14.0, 26.667, or 3 for specific test cases.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The spreadsheet engine has 35 tests. Initially 19 pass and 16 fail. The agent must reach 30+ passing tests. Bugs to fix: 1) parser.py: parse_expression handles +,-,*,/ at same precedence. Fix by splitting into parse_expression (+/-) and parse_term (*/). 2) csv_io.py: _infer_type only tries float when '.' is present. Fix by stripping whitespace first. 3) cell.py: get_numeric_value returns 0 for empty cells. Fix by returning None. 4) dependency.py: get_evaluation_order uses a single visited set causing false circular errors. Fix by using separate permanent and temporary sets. Stubs to complete: 1) evaluator.py: eval_range needs 2D range support (e.g., A1:C3). 2) functions.py: VLOOKUP needs exact and approximate match. 3) functions.py: COUNTIF needs criteria parsing ('>100', etc.). 4) functions.py: AVERAGE needs numeric-only mean skipping None. The agent must not modify files in tests/ or data/.
